name: Hungary (1956 revolution flag, "lyukas zászló")
description: Horizontal triband of red, white and green with a circular hole in the centre of the white band, where the Rákosi-era coat of arms has been cut out — the iconic 'lyukas zászló' (flag with a hole) of the 1956 Hungarian Revolution.
history: From 23 October 1956 demonstrators cut the Stalinist Rákosi coat of arms out of state flags, leaving a circular hole in the white band; the resulting 'lyukas zászló' became the visual symbol of the uprising. It was flown widely until Soviet forces crushed the revolution on 4 November 1956. The State Council briefly reinstated the historical Kossuth arms (12 November 1956) before the Kádár coat of arms was decreed on 23 May 1957.

features.role: circular cut-out where the Rákosi coat of arms had been
features.symbolism: removal of the imposed Soviet-style emblem — the defining mark of the 1956 revolution
